title: Right man for the right job
primary_topics: Michael M. Alba investiture as FEU's 11th president
sub_topics: Sampaloc to Diliman 15-centavo bus fare anecdote
Alejandro R. Roces (FEU arts and sciences dean, youngest education secretary)
Jovito R. Salonga and the 'FEU model' of legal education
Fr. Michael Nolan (FEU chaplain) and FEU Chapel construction
FEU presidents lineage (Reyes Jr., Reyes, Sta. Maria, De Jesus, Echauz)
five schools that have produced Chief Justices

stances.claim: He became a Supreme Court Justice and 21st Chief Justice because he is an FEU graduate — what he has been and will ever be he owes 'first and foremost' to Jesus Christ, second to his parents, and third to FEU and his other schools.

Dr. Michael Alba is the right man for the right job at the right time for the right institution — perceptive, knowledgeable, purpose-driven, humble, with educational ideas blending past legacies with present exigencies and future hopes.
stances.rhetorical_move: right-man / right-job / right-time / right-institution chiasmic endorsement

stances.claim: FEU taught him not only the arts and sciences, not only religious faith, not only law — it also gave him the opportunity to excel in student leadership, transforming him from 'a shy introvert in Mapa High' to an active student leader.
stances.rhetorical_move: negative-enumeration plus-something pattern + personal metamorphosis framing

institutions: National Union of Students of the Philippines

signature_phrases: the right man for the right job at the right time for the right institution
the FEU model of legal education
Non-Tamaraws will please bear with us
from a shy introvert in Mapa High to an active student leader
register_markers: Permit me, however, to modify that a little bit
decision_framework_signals: applies right-man/right-job/right-time/right-institution heuristic for institutional leadership endorsement
uses gratitude-hierarchy framing (faith → family → education) for personal autobiography
frames technological change inventory as evidence of educational transformation imperative
notable_anecdotes: Going to the UP campus before graduation with Mapa High batch mates, standing before the Oblation and promising one another to earn UP scholarships
Earning a UP scholarship but being unable to attend because parents could not afford the 15-centavo bus fare from Sampaloc to Quezon City
Salonga taking 'a personal liking' to him and teaching the FEU model (UP for basic + Harvard for master's + Yale for doctorate)
Helping Fr. Nolan build the FEU Chapel
Being the youngest and first sophomore student elected president of FEU Central Student Organization
FEU President Evangelista, of '19 years from 1952 to 1971,' seeing him often despite being only one of 36,000 students due to his student-leadership activities
